title: 10 best herbal creams for oral thrush

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with oral thrush because they contain calendula, a natural antifungal agent that inhibits the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for the infection. The anti-inflammatory properties of calendula also soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, calendula's antibacterial properties help prevent secondary infections, promoting a healthy environment for the oral cavity to recover. This natural remedy can aid in the management of oral thrush symptoms and promote faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial and antibacterial properties: These properties help to combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush, reducing the risk of spreading the infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the oral tissues, making it easier to manage symptoms such as mouth pain and difficulty swallowing.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams can help to protect the oral tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by the infection, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid and let it cool to room temperature for 1 hour.
Combine 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Blend the infused liquid with the oil and wax mixture until smooth and well combined for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely for 30 minutes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Calyxes of flowers are used to make creams for oral thrush as they have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are used in medicinal creams for oral thrush as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antifungal activity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with oral thrush because they contain antiviral and antifungal properties that combat the Candida albicans fungus that causes the condition. The cream's soothing and cooling effects can also reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with oral thrush. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to prevent the spread of the infection, promoting a healthy oral environment and supporting the recovery process. This natural remedy can be a valuable adjunct to conventional treatment.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lemon balm's antifungal properties help to inhibit the growth of Candida, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the inflammation and discomfort caused by oral thrush.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the presence of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the oral tissues affected by thrush.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help combat fungal infections and reduce inflammation in the mouth.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant have antiseptic properties that help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of shea butter and 100g of coconut oil in separate containers for melting.
Infuse 20g of melissa officinalis leaves in 200ml of jojoba oil for 2-3 hours under cold temperature.
Strain the infused jojoba oil and discard the melissa leaves to prevent contamination.
Blend the melted shea butter and coconut oil with the infused jojoba oil in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the blended cream mixture into a glass jar and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with oral thrush because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections. When applied topically to the affected area, chamomile creams can help to reduce the symptoms of oral thrush, such as redness, swelling, and irritation, and promote a healthy oral environment. Regular use can also aid in the prevention of future outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe the irritated mucous membranes in the mouth, making it easier to manage oral thrush symptoms.

Antimicrobial properties: Chamomile's antimicrobial activity helps combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush, reducing the growth of Candida albicans and promoting a healthy balance of oral flora.
Sothing and protective properties: Chamomile's soothing and protective effects help repair and shield the mouth from further irritation, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, the flowers are used to create creams that soothe oral thrush symptoms.
Roots: The roots have antifungal properties that help combat the fungal infection causing oral thrush, making them a valuable ingredient for creams.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground matricaria chamomilla powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture is smooth.
Stir in 5 drops of tea tree essential oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil into the mixture for their antifungal properties.
Pour the mixture into small, sterilized jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with oral thrush because they contain antimicrobial properties that combat fungal infections. By targeting the root cause of oral thrush, dandelion creams offer a natural and effective solution for this common health issue.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the oral tissues affected by thrush, reducing redness and discomfort.
Antifungal activity: Dandelion's natural antifungal properties help inhibit the growth of the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush, allowing the body to fight off the infection.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help protect the oral tissues from damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting a healthy healing environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in their full bloom stage for maximum potency and efficacy.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve the active ingredients.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried taraxacum officinale in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a strong herbal liquid.
Mix 10% of the infused liquid with 90% of a neutral carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil to create a final cream concentration.
Filter the cream mixture through a coffee filter to remove any sediment and achieve a smooth, uniform texture.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Leaves: Contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been found to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans.
Seeds: Contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Prepare echinacea angustifolia roots by cleaning and drying them for 2 weeks in a cool, dry place.
Mix 100g of dried echinacea roots with 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring frequently, until the beeswax dissolves completely in 30 minutes.
Add 10ml of jojoba oil and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture, stirring well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small jars, letting it cool and solidify completely before use, in 1 hour.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower plant are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, making them a potential remedy for oral thrush.
Fruits: The seeds of the Kansas coneflower plant contain compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial in treating oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal properties. The main ingredient, methyl salicylate, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers at dawn, taking 1/2 cup of fresh material per 100g cream recipe.
Dry the harvested gaultheria procumbias leaves and flowers in a warm place for 7 to 10 days, turning occasionally.
Crush 1 tablespoon of dried gaultheria procumbias leaves and flowers and combine with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax mixture in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 20 to 30 minutes or until smooth.
Remove the gaultheria procumbias and beeswax mixture from heat, let it cool and solidify before storing in airtight containers.

Rhizomes: Contain salicylic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause oral thrush, promoting healing and preventing recurrence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal creams helps with oral thrush because they contain extracts of the plant Prunella vulgaris, which has antimicrobial properties that can target and eliminate the fungal overgrowth that causes thrush. The cream's soothing and anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm irritated mucous membranes and reduce discomfort. remedy_recipe: Gather 30-40 grams of fresh prunella vulgaris leaves and flowers and dry them for 2-3 hours in a low-temperature oven at 35°c.
Crush 10 grams of dried prunella vulgaris into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 30 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat to melt the beeswax.
Combine the crushed prunella vulgaris powder with the melted beeswax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes before use.

remedy_recipe: Combine 30 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves with 250 milliliters of oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours, then strain it through cheesecloth to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of coconut oil and add 10 milliliters of the infused oil.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the cream into containers and let it cool completely before using.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Infuse 100g of dried achillea millefolium in 1l of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion and mix it with 100g of coconut oil and 100g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160°f - 180°f for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the hot mixture into sterile containers and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

Strain the infusion and let it cool to room temperature, then add 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel to enhance moisturizing properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of fresh foeniculum vulgare seeds or 20-25 grams of dried seeds.
Chop the foeniculum vulgare seeds into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or a food processor.
Mix the chopped seeds with 20 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

Stir the mixture every 5 minutes to ensure even melting and incorporation of ingredients for 10-15 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Extract eucalyptus globulus essential oil by steam distillation and measure 10ml of the obtained oil.
Prepare an infusion by steeping 2 tablespoons of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 8oz of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the infusion and combine it with the extracted eucalyptus globulus essential oil, 1 teaspoon of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth and creamy, approximately 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest eriobotrya japonica leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days to preserve its properties.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried eriobotrya japonica in 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler for 2-3 hours.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth and creamy texture.
Pour the mixture into small glass containers and let it cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with oral thrush because of its soothing and antimicrobial properties. The marshmallow root extract in these creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill Candida, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The cream also helps to restore the mucous membranes' natural pH balance and promote healing, making it a gentle and effective treatment option for oral thrush. This can provide relief for individuals experiencing symptoms such as pain, discomfort, and difficulty swallowing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: Marshmallow creams help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from the discomfort and pain caused by oral thrush.

Mucilaginous properties: Marshmallow creams contain mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to shield them from further irritation and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial and antifungal properties: Marshmallow creams contain compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial pathogens, including Candida albicans, which is the primary cause of oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Contains mucilages that soothe and protect oral mucous membranes.
Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antifungal properties.
Buds: High in saponins that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of althaea officinalis root powder and add 500ml of distilled water to create a decoction.
Infuse the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight to allow the plant's active compounds to release.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 100g of beeswax, 100g of coconut oil, and 100g of shea butter with the strained decoction to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into containers and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to set and thicken before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for oral thrush are topical treatments that harness the antifungal and antibacterial properties of natural ingredients to combat oral thrush. These creams, often containing tea tree oil, aloe vera, or caprylic acid, help soothe and heal the mucous membranes, reducing discomfort and inflammation. Products like Orajel or Thrush Shield can provide relief from oral thrush symptoms, improving daily life for individuals with the condition, allowing for more comfortable eating, speaking, and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oral thrush with herbal creams is to apply aloe vera or tea tree oil cream directly to the affected area. These creams have antifungal properties that help combat the infection. Additionally, aloe vera soothes and calms the mucous membranes, while tea tree oil's antibacterial properties reduce inflammation and promote healing. Apply 2-3 times a day for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oral thrush that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections such as vaginal yeast infections, diaper rash, and athlete's foot. These conditions are caused by overgrowth of fungi on the skin or mucous membranes, and herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem oil can help to control the growth of fungi and soothe the affected area.
